title: acer orientale var. cuneifolium
intro: Acer orientale var. cuneifolium, also known as the Manchurian maple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to China and the Korean Peninsula. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of the common cold. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of various compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Acer orientale var. cuneifolium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which are often used to treat respiratory, gastrointestinal, and dermatological conditions.
